Navigation.details: Outputs the full absolute path of the current directory.
Lists files and directories in the current working directory without hidden files.
Lists all files (including hidden ones starting with '.') in long format, showing permissions, ownership, size, and timestamps.
Navigation.example:
$ ls -la
drwxr-xr-x 2 user user 4096 Mar 17 10:00 .
-rw-r--r-- 1 user user  120 Mar 17 10:00 .env

Navigation.details: Changes the current working directory to the specified path.
Navigates directly to the current user's home directory.
Navigates to the directory you were previously in (like a back button).
Recursively lists directory contents in a tree-like format.

File Operations.details: Copies a file from the source path to the destination path.
Recursively copies a directory and all of its contents.
Moves a file or directory. Also used to rename files if the destination is in the same directory.
Permanently removes a file. Does not work on directories.
Recursively and forcefully removes a directory and all its contents without prompting. Extremely dangerous!
Creates an empty file, or updates the modification timestamp if the file already exists.
Creates a symbolic link (shortcut) pointing to the source file or directory.

File Operations.desc: Get the absolute path of a file
File Operations.details: Prints the resolved absolute path of a file, resolving any symbolic links or relative paths.
Changes file permissions. 755 gives read/write/execute to owner, and read/execute to group and others.
Changes the owner and group of a file or directory.
Creates a compressed gzipped tarball archive of a directory.
Extracts a gzipped tarball archive into the current directory.
Outputs the last 10 lines of a file and actively monitors it for new appended lines. Great for logs.
Opens a file in an interactive pager, allowing you to scroll up and down without loading the entire file into memory.

Search.desc: Search for a pattern in a file
Search.details: Searches for a specific text pattern or regular expression inside a file.
Recursively searches for a text pattern through all files in the current directory and subdirectories.
Searches the directory tree for files matching a specific name pattern.

Search.desc: Find a file quickly using the database
Search.details: Quickly finds files by name using a pre-built database (faster than find but may not show newly created files).
A powerful text-processing language. This basic example prints specific columns from text output.
Stream editor. Used to perform basic text transformations, like replacing all occurrences of a string.

Processes.details: Lists all running processes on the system, showing user, PID, CPU, memory, and command.
Lists all running processes using the standard System V format.

Processes.desc: List all processes with terminal attached
Processes.details: Lists all processes with a terminal attached.
Provides a dynamic, real-time view of running processes and system resource usage.
An interactive and colorful alternative to top, allowing easier process management (often requires installation).
Sends a TERM signal to gracefully terminate a process using its Process ID.

Processes.desc: Force kill a process by PID
Processes.details: Sends a KILL signal to forcefully terminate a process immediately. Only use if graceful kill fails.
Terminates all processes matching the specified name.

Networking.details: Sends ICMP echo requests to check network connectivity and measure latency to a host.
Transfers data from or to a server using supported protocols. Great for testing APIs.

Networking.desc: Download a file from a URL
Networking.details: Downloads files from the web directly to your current directory.

Networking.desc: Log in to a remote host via SSH
Networking.details: Logs into a remote machine securely over an encrypted connection.
Securely copies files between local and remote hosts using SSH.
Synchronizes files and directories between two locations. It's fast and resumes interrupted transfers.

Networking.desc: Show IP addresses and network interfaces
Networking.details: Displays IP addresses, network interfaces, and routing information.

Pipes & Redirection.desc: Pass output of cmd1 as input to cmd2 (Pipe)
Pipes & Redirection.details: Pipes the standard output of the first command directly into the standard input of the second command.

Pipes & Redirection.desc: Page through a long list of files
Pipes & Redirection.details: Pipes a long directory listing into the 'less' pager so you can scroll through it.
Pipes the list of all processes into grep to filter and find a specific running app.
Redirects the standard output of a command into a file, overwriting its existing contents.
Redirects standard output and appends it to the end of a file.

Pipes & Redirection.desc: Run a command and discard all output
Pipes & Redirection.details: Redirects both standard output and standard error to the black hole, silencing the command completely.

System Info.details: Displays the amount of available disk space on file systems in human-readable format (MB, GB).
Calculates and displays the total disk usage of a specific directory.
Shows the total amount of free and used physical and swap memory in the system (in Megabytes).
Prints all system information including kernel name, network node hostname, kernel release, and machine hardware name.
